Title: A co-opted steroid synthesis gene, maintained in sorghum but not maize, is associated with a divergence in leaf wax chemistry
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: oxidosqualene cyclase / mixed hopane triterpenoid synthase (primarily simiarenol synthase)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Simiarenol; fernenol; isoarborinol; likely fernenone and isoarborinone as downstream oxidized derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: adult leaf cuticular wax, including epicuticular and intracuticular wax layers on both adaxial and abaxial surfaces
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cuticular wax components associated with strengthening the cuticle and helping maintain the leaf water barrier at high temperatures, contributing to drought/heat tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: hopane triterpenoid branch of cuticular wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms major cuticular triterpenol products and likely supplies precursors for corresponding ketones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene into a mixed set of hopane-type triterpenols, predominantly simiarenol, plus fernenol and isoarborinol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a key control/product-determining step for the majority of sorghum leaf triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes accumulation of the majority of sorghum adult-leaf cuticular triterpenoids; likely accounts for about 64% of surface triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: public leaf RNA-seq expression mining; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; GC-MS product identification; comparative genomics/synteny; phylogenetic analysis; maize stop-codon verification by PCR/sequencing; leaf wax metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: one of the highest expressed sorghum OSC genes in leaves; highly expressed through developmental stages including rapid leaf growth and wax synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: one of the highest expressed OSCs in sorghum leaves across public RNA-seq datasets
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: juvenile-to-adult transition; adult leaves; greenhouse and field conditions; elevated temperature context for inferred barrier function
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from NCBI Sequence Read Archive sorghum leaf datasets (50 filtered samples); Dryad raw data for Figs. 1-4 and 6
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: literature discussed in paper suggests cuticular triterpenoids may increase water permeability or have negligible barrier role at non-elevated temperatures, implying possible temperature-dependent tradeoff
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: prime candidate to engineer or introgress triterpenoid-rich cuticles for improved heat-tolerant water barriers; proposed resurrection of corresponding function in maize
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: identified as a neofunctionalized steroid biosynthesis gene maintained in sorghum but disrupted in maize; major triterpenoid synthase for sorghum adult leaves; product ratio in yeast approximately 2:6:1 for fernenol:simiarenol:isoarborinol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sobic.008G142400.1 encodes a mixed hopane triterpenoid synthase that produces fernenol, simiarenol, and isoarborinol in yeast. 2. Its products match the major triterpenoid alcohols on adult sorghum leaf surfaces, and the gene likely contributes to the majority of sorghum cuticular triterpenoids. 3. It is syntenic to disrupted maize remnants, supporting maintenance in sorghum but loss of function in maize after ancestral neofunctionalization from a sterol-pathway gene.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cuticular wax triterpenoids potentially contributing to cuticle properties
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: amyrin branch of cuticular wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to a mixed product set of β-amyrin and α-amyrin at about a 1:1 ratio
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: product-determining step for amyrin branch; broader rate-limiting role not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables biosynthesis of the amyrin triterpenols found on sorghum adult leaf wax
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: public leaf RNA-seq expression mining; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; GC-MS product identification; leaf wax metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: one of the higher expressed sorghum OSC genes in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: one of the higher expressed OSCs in sorghum leaves across public RNA-seq datasets
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: adult leaf stage after juvenile-to-adult transition
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from NCBI Sequence Read Archive sorghum leaf datasets (50 filtered samples); Dryad raw data for Figs. 1-4 and 6
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for engineering specific amyrin-containing wax chemistry, likely in combination with hopane-triterpenoid synthase genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: together with Sobic.008G142400.1 generates the main triterpenol products detected on sorghum leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sobic.007G085800.1 is highly expressed in sorghum leaves among OSC candidates. 2. Yeast expression showed it produces β-amyrin and α-amyrin in a roughly 1:1 ratio. 3. It contributes to the major amyrin-type triterpenols present in adult sorghum leaf wax.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular sterol pathway, not reported as leaf wax product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor to plant steroid-type hormones and sterols
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: committed/product-forming cyclization step in sterol branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes for common precursor pool rather than directly forming leaf wax triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to cycloartenol, a precursor for plant sterol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: identified as a functional cycloartenol synthase, supporting ancestral sterol-pathway origin of specialized sorghum wax triterpenoid synthases
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: public leaf RNA-seq expression mining; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; GC-MS product identification; phylogenetic/synteny analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: among the higher expressed sorghum OSC genes in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: among the higher expressed OSCs in sorghum leaves across public RNA-seq datasets
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from NCBI Sequence Read Archive sorghum leaf datasets (50 filtered samples)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: mainly provides evolutionary and pathway context rather than a direct wax engineering target in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: not implicated as producing sorghum leaf surface triterpenoids; used to support the conclusion that specialized cuticular triterpenoid synthases arose by neofunctionalization from a sterol biosynthesis gene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sobic.004G037200.1 is a leaf-expressed sorghum OSC candidate. 2. Yeast expression showed it produces cycloartenol rather than cuticular wax triterpenoids. 3. Its function supports an ancestral sterol biosynthesis origin for the neofunctionalized sorghum wax triterpenoid synthase lineage.
